assumption: Our primary buyers are engineering managers, not VPs
evidence_at_creation: Based on 20 discovery calls in Q3 2025 — 18/20 champions were engineering managers
new_evidence: Win/loss analysis Q2 2026: 6 of last 10 enterprise wins had VP of Engineering as economic buyer, not manager

assumption: Customers will not pay more than $500/month in SMB segment
evidence_at_creation: Survey of 30 SMB prospects in 2025 — median WTP was $300/month
new_evidence: 3 SMB customers on $600/mo plan (launched Q1 2026). No churn after 6 months. N=3 so limited confidence.

assumption: G2 reviews are the primary discovery channel for mid-market buyers
evidence_at_creation: Survey: 14/20 mid-market buyers said they checked G2 during evaluation
new_evidence: New survey (n=40, Q2 2026): G2 cited by 35%, peer referral cited by 42%. G2 still important but not primary.

assumption: Integration quality is a top-3 purchase criterion for all segments
evidence_at_creation: Feature ranking survey 2025 — integrations ranked #2 overall
new_evidence: Feature ranking repeated Q1 2026 — integrations still #2 overall but #4 for SMB segment specifically

assumption: The market will not see an AI-native entrant with traction before 2027
evidence_at_creation: No credible AI-native PM tool with >500 customers as of 2025
new_evidence: NewEntrant Co raised YC seed in July 2026. 200 waitlist signups. Too early to assess traction.
